headline: Chip stocks rebound after bullish outlooks from Micron, Qualcomm
summary:
STORY: Chip stocks are seeing a rebound after bullish outlooks from U.S. firms Micron Technology and Qualcomm.

South Korea’s tech-heavy Kospi index jumped around 5% in early trade Thursday.

Chip giant SK Hynix gained around 10% by mid-morning.

The rally came after days of sharp declines for global tech stocks, sparked by concerns over the sky-high valuations for AI-related shares.

:: Micron

Those worries eased late Wednesday when Micron said customers had committed to spend $22 billion on its chips.

It also forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations, sending its shares 12% higher in after-hours trade.

Landsberg Bennett Chief Investment Officer Michael Landsberg says the AI boom has been transformational for memory chip makers like Micron:

“What’s happened in this space has been, typically this used to be more of a commodity, and so what’s happened now with AI, a lot of these chips are needed to make AI work faster and better so the demand has really been strong across the entire sector.”

Also after the bell, Qualcomm said it expected $15 billion in sales for its data center business by 2029.

That’s as it seeks to move beyond its core market of chips for smartphones and focus on AI.

:: SK Hynix

Wednesday also saw SK Hynix reveal plans for a major Wall Street stock listing that may raise close to $30 billion.

That could make it the second-biggest share sale ever, coming just after the record IPO by Elon Musk’s rocket firm SpaceX.

content.title: Chip stocks rebound after bullish outlooks from Micron, Qualcomm

headline: Seoul stocks soar in Asia tech rally after Micron blowout forecast
summary: South Korea's Kospi soared more than five percent in a tech-led surge across Asia on Thursday as a forecast-busting earnings report from US chip titan Micron helped traders brush off their latest worries about the AI investment boom.SK hynix jumped around 10 percent and Samsung more than five percent, pushing up the Kospi almost six percent at one point.

headline: Why Seagate (STX) Shares Are Getting Obliterated Today
summary: Shares of data storage manufacturer Seagate (NASDAQ:STX) fell 7.1% in the afternoon session after a leverage-driven rout in South Korean chipmakers, renewed doubts about debt-funded AI capital spending, and a hawkish repricing of Fed rate expectations hit the year's most crowded trade, leaving the whole complex hostage to Micron's earnings after the close.

headline: Korean chip shares surge on Micron's blowout earnings, SK Hynix's US listing plan
summary: South Korean semiconductor shares rallied on Thursday after U.S. memory chipmaker Micron Technology's quarterly results and forecast beat expectations, ‌boosting optimism over AI-driven chip shortages. South Korea's SK Hynix also said on Wednesday it plans ‌to raise up to $29.4 billion through a U.S. stock market listing, boosting investor expectations of a reduced valuation gap between ​the chipmaker and its smaller U.S. rival Micron. Shares of SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics rose as much as 11.6% and 6.2%, respectively, in early trade, tracking a rally in U.S. chip stocks.

content.title: Korean chip shares surge on Micron's blowout earnings, SK Hynix's US listing plan
content.summary: South Korean semiconductor shares rallied on Thursday after U.S. memory chipmaker Micron Technology's quarterly results and forecast beat expectations, ‌boosting optimism over AI-driven chip shortages. South Korea's SK Hynix also said on Wednesday it plans ‌to raise up to $29.4 billion through a U.S. stock market listing, boosting investor expectations of a reduced valuation gap between ​the chipmaker and its smaller U.S. rival Micron. Shares of SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics rose as much as 11.6% and 6.2%, respectively, in early trade, tracking a rally in U.S. chip stocks.
